algorithms.title: Suspected Toxic Exposure Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing and managing a suspected poisoning or overdose.
steps: Ensure scene safety, especially for exposures involving unknown chemicals or gases.
Support airway, breathing, and circulation as the priority over exact substance identification.
Identify the substance, amount, and time of exposure when possible; bring containers/pills to the hospital.
Check blood glucose regardless of a presumed toxic cause.
Recognize toxidrome patterns (opioid, sympathomimetic, anticholinergic, cholinergic) to guide urgency and specific interventions (e.g., naloxone for opioid toxidrome per protocol).
Transport promptly with all identified substances/containers accompanying the patient.

algorithms.title: Heat and Cold Environmental Emergency Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing and managing heat-related and cold-related environmental emergencies.
steps: Remove the patient from the ongoing environmental extreme.
Assess mental status and estimate severity along the relevant spectrum (heat cramps → exhaustion → stroke; mild → moderate → severe hypothermia).
For heat illness: if altered mental status plus significantly elevated core temperature is present, treat as heat stroke — begin aggressive cooling and rapid transport regardless of sweating status.
For milder heat illness (cramps, exhaustion): rest, cooling, and fluids (oral if tolerated and alert).
For cold illness: handle the patient gently, especially if severe hypothermia is suspected, given arrhythmia risk with rough movement.
Apply passive/active external rewarming appropriate to severity.
For a hypothermic patient in apparent cardiac arrest: provide resuscitation per protocol rather than presuming futility, since hypothermia can mimic death.
Transport promptly with a lower threshold for more severe classifications on either spectrum.
